tldr: Apify runs scrapers; Cortex is an internal developer portal. The realistic migration is to register Apify actors as services in Cortex for ownership and scorecards, not to replace the scraping runtime.
why_switch: Track ownership and on-call for scraping services
Apply maturity scorecards across all services including scrapers
Centralize discoverability with the rest of engineering
Pull GitHub, cloud, and PagerDuty signals into one view
what_to_export: Actor name, description, and owning team
export_method: Use the Apify API or CLI to list all actors, schedules, and storage stores, then for each actor capture the metadata you need in Cortex: name, repo, description, team, lifecycle, runtime dashboard URL. Datasets and run history do not need to be exported - they stay in Apify or move with the runtime separately. Pull team ownership from your HR or IDP system rather than from Apify, since Apify's view of teams is usually partial. Save the export as JSON or a CSV that maps cleanly onto Cortex service fields. Re-run after any actor churn so the import reflects current state.
import_method: Cortex models services through YAML descriptors (commonly cortex.yaml) checked into each repo and through API/CLI imports. For each actor, create a service entry with owner, tier, lifecycle, links (repo, runtime, runbook), and tags. Use the Cortex API or CLI to bulk-create entries from your exported JSON. Connect Cortex to GitHub for repo signals, to your cloud provider for infra signals, and to PagerDuty or Opsgenie for on-call. Add scorecards only after the catalog is accurate - rolling them out too early creates noise. Validate by spot-checking 10-20 services for accurate ownership and working links.
gotchas: Cortex does not run scrapers - the runtime stays on Apify or wherever you replatform
Per-seat pricing means cost scales with engineering headcount, not service count
Scorecards need stakeholder buy-in or they decay into noise
GitHub repo for Cortex (cortexlabs) is a different unrelated project; rely on the SaaS product
Catalog quality depends on accurate ownership - budget time for cleanup
Integrations with cloud and on-call require admin access to set up

email.subject: Registering Apify actors in Cortex over the next 14 days
email.body:
Hi,

We are adding our Apify actors to Cortex as service entries so we can track ownership, maturity, and on-call alongside the rest of our services. Apify keeps running the actors - this is about visibility, not replatforming.

You will see a cortex.yaml descriptor land in each actor repo, plus a one-time backfill from our existing Apify metadata. We will share a validation list so you can confirm team and lifecycle are correct. Scorecards roll out only after the catalog is clean.

No action required this week. Reply if your team owns scrapers we should prioritize in the validation pass.

Thanks,
Your customer
